title: Minty Leaf Vietnamese King Prawns Wrap
description: A spicy, creamy brunch-style Vietnamese wrap that pairs fresh raw king prawns with a tangy lime-coconut sauce, crisp mint leaves and crunchy vegetables, all wrapped in a fresh ti leaf.
instructions: Rinse the king prawns under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Whisk together lime juice, coconut milk, fish sauce, chili flakes, and sweet potato starch until the starch is fully dissolved to form a creamy sauce.
Gently fold the raw prawns into the sauce, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 15 minutes to let the flavors mingle.
While the prawns marinate, blanch the mint, cucumber, carrot, and bell pepper in boiling water for 30 seconds, then plunge them into ice water to keep them crisp.
Pat the vegetables dry with a towel and arrange a handful of each on top of a softened ti leaf.
Add a spoonful of the chilled prawn sauce and a few raw prawns onto the vegetable bed.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro over the top for extra aroma.
Roll the ti leaf tightly around the fillings, sealing the edge with a drop of water to keep it closed, and serve immediately.
